script_text: Every vowel has two voices: short and long.
Short sounds are quick. Long sounds say the letter's name.
The word cat has a short A.
The word cake has a long A.
Long A says AY. It says its name!
A silent E at the end often signals a long vowel.
Ask: does the vowel say its name? Long. Or does it make a quick sound? Short.
Now you are ready to tell long from short vowels!
